Polished Mithril Torque  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK
AC: 7
WIS: +13 INT: +13
WT: 4.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD MNK BRD ROG BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Polished Mithril Torque
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK
AC: 7
WIS: +13 INT: +13
WT: 4.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD MNK BRD ROG BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Wyldkeel Bladeflayer
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-19 03:17:25
Page Updated:Tue Apr 11th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 50
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Nagafen's Lair
NPC Name
King Tranix


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Nagafen's Lair


Uploaded April 12th, 2025 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
good for all
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2005 at 2:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341360172
16 posts
I personally think it should be ALL/ALL. No matter what class you are, you can still use armor that has stats like this. I have a char of almost every class there is and I have yet to find one that could not use this item. Melee for one can use it to train faster, which goes for all the classes...and any caster type can use the Int or Wis, and the ac isnt bad either. Anyone who says any class cannot use this is cracked, and overpowered? An item like this would let me trade out a different armor piece that has wis on it for agility, stamina, dexterity, charisma, or even strength for my cleric. there was a post stating that we only had to concentrate on 1 stat....that poster cant have been playing for very long at that time. Every stat ties in to the survivability of your character, and thus the group. My cleric has low agility, and that is what im concentrating on now. It hurts to get hit and its best to avoid it. And since clerics get hit, the stamina i could get on another item that i could get w/o worrying about losing wis would be a major help. Im tired of dying more times than a ranger, but at least i can rez myself.
sortof jumbled but i hope you can get the gist of the message.
▲ Page top
By the guy who took the pic...

10 posts
too over-powered for pure casters. 13int/wis? holy ******* thinks the pure casters, on such an easy target for the reward.

that's why it's nice for mid-level knights. :)

▲ Page top
Damn
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2002 at 2:50 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253120327
27 posts
I was on a Naggy Raid today and when ML said a 13 wis neck item dropped,I was so excited.Then he said Hybrid only.WTF?I would've killed for this thing.../sigh
▲ Page top
RE: Damn
QuoteReply
# Jan 09 2003 at 7:01 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256413352
17 posts
For hybrids, getting melee stats and caster stats up at the same time involves a lot of tradeoffs. I play an SK, but I can just imagine a Pally's mana bar getting wiped out completely when they use their newest rezz spell. For SKs like myself, fear kiting sucks mana like crazy too. Basically, this item lets us focus on other stats than int or Wiz. The AC is a big bonus too over the no AC Velium Saphire or Ruby necklaces.

I got one right befor patch tonight on Saryrn. Its my first no drop piece that I have ever been psyched about, and if casters could equip it, I would have felt like a jerk rolling on it...

And if you think that the wrong classes get this item, go check the Plane of Hate bestiary and see the random stuff that Innorukk drops...it'll put this item in perspective.

Casters

# Mar 19 2002 at 1:29 AM Rating: Default

25 posts
Pure Casters can't wear this for the same reason rogues can't use primal velium war lances. It would be too powerful. At least, that's what I'd assume. C'est la vie. This item is only useful for hybrids a bit, though it is very useful for trade skilling... my rogue would love it for that purpose.

Jarkell Ravensteel

I play a ranger and I want this item. 13 wis and 7 ac to me seems great. I guess when you have good str, good agi, and good dex you want to move on to stats like ac and wisdom.

RE: Wrong Classes

# Jan 15 2003 at 7:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780482351
26 posts
Minadin, you are absolutely correct. This is an excellent piece for anyone who's working on trade skills. Sadly, very few people realize this, and most of those who do don't care. So, the chances are that a warrior would be laughed at if he/she wanted to roll on one for working up tradeskills, but in all honestly it would be well worth it.
▲ Page top
WTF????
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 12:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
How long has this game been out and you people still argue why this item wasn't geared towards casters?

Anyway, melee should go strictly AC from lvl 1 up till they start doing ubermobs-where-high-AC-meets-diminishing-returns then they go for resists and HPs.
▲ Page top
Melee Based?
QuoteReply
# Jul 30 2001 at 5:16 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I think at any given time each person wants more for their character, that is understandable. But, I would like to stand up for us melee types and make an important distinction from casters: i.e., a caster's damage production is more level driven than equipment driven. Yes, the more mana a caster has the more overall damage they can do. However, a caster at lvl 40 is going to have the same spells available to him/her that other casters at that lvl have. Some spells are NPC drop, but most if not nearly all of those can be purchased easy enough. Moreover, a caster needs only trully worry about ONE stat increase (either int or wis). Most of my non-twinked caster friends had int or wis near or over 200 in the early or mid thirties. Compare to a melee. First, a melee of same class and race at lvl 40 can be way out of line with another melee at lvl 40 of the same class and race because of difference in weapon type or equipment types. Several levels worth of difference actually from what I have seen in the PvP arena. Case in point, AC is not built into most classes (not sure of monks and what they have going) and, try boosting agi, dex and str at the same time too. A melee has to make a decision what they are looking for (trade AC for HP increase?). A caster only needs to ask one question: will that item I want to purchase give me more int/wis than what I have? I am not saying casters are better, or that melees don't have the opportunity to make themselves useful. What I am saying is please keep a perspective. PS: the game changes as you reach level 50 and above. I don't have much experience at those levels so my opinions are only based from my experiences there down.
▲ Page top
RE: Melee Based?
QuoteReply
# Jan 21 2003 at 9:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781140857
View User Forum
68 posts
Sounds about right, only it comes down to balancing int/wis, hp/mana, and saves.
▲ Page top
Uber Ranger Item
QuoteReply
# Jul 12 2001 at 12:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763737927
8 posts
Ok this is great for Rangers. If it drops... GIVE IT TO THE RANGER!!! The palys and sks need more ac as its been said. They do more tanking than the ranger. The ranger is a dmg doer not reciever plain and simple. The wis will help out his damage doin with the spells he'll be casting. Wether it's dd's or whatever, if the paly or sk have it while tanking, then they wont get many spells off because of the mob whomping on them. So dont be greedy and help out the rangers, they need all the help they can get with Verant screwin em at every turn.

# Jul 11 2001 at 1:57 AM Rating: Default

Anonymous
Funny thing about game balance is that it is way too far in the direction of the melees right now,so right now it wouldnt really be overpowered anymore that much, but back when this came out it would have been way too powerful
▲ Page top
RE: Not really anymore
QuoteReply
# Nov 09 2001 at 7:25 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592248132329
41 posts
You really think game balance favors melees? i beg to differ but that is just not correct. if you go to everquest.com you will find info on the alliance server, where they keep track of who kills who. you want to guess who is on top all the time? halfling druids....with the ocasional DE wizard here and there...no tanks at all. and really when was the last time you heard of a tank "quading" yellows? like a druids (mostly) and other casters? give me one yellow and ill show you my back side as i run for the zone! LOL
▲ Page top
RE: Not really anymore
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2001 at 8:42 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766507531
36 posts
Druids can't quad yellows, the mobs resist barely often enough that there is no way they will have the mana. There are a few exceptions, like people at level 34 with velious quest armor and a TON of mana items, but for the most part, a druid with a wis of 200 and little else in the way of mana items can't even touch quadding except on low blues, plain and simple.

You say "they can med up enough to cast another nuke" Snare (and Ensnare, for that matter) doesn't last forever, and it's difficult to resnare a group of mobs and line them back up for quadding, especially when you are oom. Now, a 34 druid with a C2, insight, and GoM CAN quad the docks in butcher, I've done it before, but that's not TRUE soloing, and it was STILL difficult.
▲ Page top
RE: Not really anymore
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2001 at 4:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591773697805
11 posts
This is probably the worst post I've ever seen Essar....I signed up just so I could explain how wrong you are. First of all you MUST be at least lvl 42 to receive Clarity 2 so there is no way your druid could have had it. Second ensnare lasts 13min so it practically does last forever. If you don't kill your mob in 13min you're probably oom and zoning by that time. Third its not hard to resnare mobs as a lvl 45 druid with Elder Spiritists Gaunts have clickable snare which is manaless and they are very cheap for that matter. Fourth druid can quad yellows as I myself had in Eastern Wastes. I think I would know a little more then a 34 druid.

Troggy Treehobbit
49 Druid of Karana
Bristlebane

Edited, Sun Dec 30 16:33:15 2001

no, its not very useful for a sk or pally. the AC is extremely low. # Jun 11 2001 at 3:36 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755359730
Scholar
60 posts
13 wisdom or intelligence - how many mana is that...i'm not doing the math but its ALOT. As a paladin I'm expected to heal myself and others and Sks are expected to use their spells to use spell to increase damage and fear etc, but unless they are Dark Elves are pretty intelligence poor. Rangers have some fine nukes, dots, snare, and buffs but not alot of wisdom. This quantity of wisdom would surely improve those abilities. I'd gladly wear the thing even if it were 0 AC. This item rocks for a hybrid.

i don't know what lvl you are, Galadril, but as the proud owner of a 47 cleric, i must say that if a pally is expected to keep the group healed, there is something SERIOUSLY wrong. while it's true that pallies can make decent PL'ers, their heal is far too weak to be relied upon as the main source of life if you're fighting in places like this.

# Jan 21 2003 at 9:17 PM Rating: Decent

68 posts
On raids, paladins use group heals to top off the health of nearby characters who are not the ones getting beat on. Clerics are usually completely tied up with healing the MT, and paladins make all the main healers' jobs that much easier.
